steps: Go to https://unsplash.com/developers and click 'Register as a developer'
Sign in with your Unsplash account (or create one for free)
Accept the API usage guidelines and terms
Fill in the application name and description
After creation, scroll down to find your 'Access Key' (Client ID) and 'Secret key'
Use the Access Key as a Client-ID in the Authorization header: Authorization: Client-ID <access_key>

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Access Key (Client ID)
credential_fields.description: Unsplash Access Key used as Client-ID in the Authorization header

notes: Free tier: 50 requests/hour in development mode
Request production approval to get 5,000 requests/hour — submit screenshots of your implementation
The Access Key is used for read-only requests (Authorization: Client-ID <key>)
OAuth 2.0 (using client_id + client_secret) is required for write operations like managing collections
You must credit photographers and link back to Unsplash per the API guidelines

test_endpoint.description: List curated editorial photos from Unsplash
